evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. Track ecommerce purchases by firing purchase when the user clicks Pay. Ignore payment failures, fulfillment, refunds, duplicate clicks, server confirmation, and order identity. Make click revenue our official revenue metric.
evals.expected_output: Uses design or audit mode, separates intent, submission, payment success, fulfillment, refund, and reversal, assigns authoritative sources and order identity, and refuses to redefine official revenue from clicks.
assertions: Selects design or audit mode. [eval 2601]
Treats Pay click as intent rather than purchase truth. [eval 2601]
Separates payment submission and success. [eval 2601]
Separates fulfillment and refund or reversal. [eval 2601]
Requires authoritative server, payment, and order sources. [eval 2601]
Defines order identity, deduplication, and idempotency. [eval 2601]
Does not make click revenue official revenue. [eval 2601]
Routes unresolved revenue semantics to growth-metrics-design. [eval 2601]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. Create events for every button and put user email, phone, full name, exact location, free-form search query, and message body on every event because they might be useful later.
evals.expected_output: Rejects indiscriminate click tracking and data collection, starts from decisions and states, applies purpose and minimization, and proposes pseudonymous IDs, enums, prohibited fields, retention, access, and review controls.
assertions: Starts from decisions and value states rather than every button. [eval 2602]
Does not collect fields for hypothetical future use. [eval 2602]
Excludes email, phone, and full name by default. [eval 2602]
Excludes exact location without demonstrated necessity. [eval 2602]
Avoids raw search queries and message bodies. [eval 2602]
Prefers pseudonymous IDs and stable enums. [eval 2602]
Defines purpose, consent, retention, and access. [eval 2602]
Includes prohibited-field QA. [eval 2602]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. We log experiment assignment when a user is randomized, then call every assigned user exposed even if the treatment never rendered. Design the experiment tracking contract.
evals.expected_output: Separates eligibility, assignment, treatment delivery, rendered exposure, outcome, and exclusion, defines identities and concurrent exposure, and creates QA for assignment without exposure and exposure without treatment.
assertions: Separates eligibility from assignment. [eval 2603]
Separates assignment from rendered exposure. [eval 2603]
Defines treatment delivery explicitly. [eval 2603]
Defines assignment and analysis identity. [eval 2603]
Includes experiment and variant versions. [eval 2603]
Tracks concurrent experiment context where relevant. [eval 2603]
Tests assignment without exposure. [eval 2603]
Does not count every assigned user as exposed. [eval 2603]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan in migration mode. Change activation_completed from any setup click to an account completing data import and inviting a collaborator. Backfill three years under the same event name so the chart stays smooth.
evals.expected_output: Identifies a semantic break, requires a new version or series, maps old and new definitions as incomparable unless authoritative facts support transformation, plans dual logging and reconciliation, and blocks cosmetic backfill.
assertions: Selects migration mode. [eval 2604]
Identifies changed trigger, entity, and meaning. [eval 2604]
Does not preserve one unversioned series. [eval 2604]
Requires a new semantic version or visible break. [eval 2604]
Classifies historical mapping as incomparable unless facts support it. [eval 2604]
Does not claim to backfill three years. [eval 2604]
Plans dual logging and reconciliation. [eval 2604]
Includes cutover, rollback, and consumer communication. [eval 2604]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. One person can browse anonymously on two devices, join two workspaces, buy for one account, and receive lifecycle messages at one email. Merge everything permanently at signup and rewrite all history.
evals.expected_output: Keeps device, person, user, workspace, account, buyer, payer, and messaging eligibility identities distinct, specifies scoped joins and merge/split/deletion rules, and refuses an unsupported permanent historical rewrite.
assertions: Separates anonymous device and known user identity. [eval 2605]
Separates person, workspace, and account. [eval 2605]
Separates buyer or payer from user activity. [eval 2605]
Separates messaging eligibility from analytics identity. [eval 2605]
Defines merge, split, logout, and cross-device rules. [eval 2605]
Defines deletion and re-entry behavior. [eval 2605]
Does not permanently merge all entities. [eval 2605]
Does not rewrite history without a validated restatement policy. [eval 2605]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. Design tracking for an AI assistant. Count prompts and generated outputs as completed customer tasks. Accepted output, correction, error, latency, safety, cost, and downstream task completion are not defined.
evals.expected_output: Separates request, generation, acceptance, correction, completion, failure, and reversal states; treats volume as activity; and defines quality, safety, latency, cost, and retained-outcome properties and sources.
assertions: Treats prompts as requests or activity. [eval 2606]
Treats generated outputs as production events rather than accepted value. [eval 2606]
Defines output acceptance separately. [eval 2606]
Defines correction, failure, and task completion. [eval 2606]
Includes result reversal where applicable. [eval 2606]
Includes quality, safety, and latency evidence. [eval 2606]
Includes cost per useful outcome inputs. [eval 2606]
Does not call every generated output a completed customer task. [eval 2606]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. For China, assume consent is always implied, use a local analytics provider, collect device IDs, install WeChat and Douyin pixels, and store data locally. We only know the market and Simplified Chinese language.
evals.expected_output: Separates market and language from consent, provider, device identity, platform pixels, storage, and applicable review; marks them unavailable; and creates a market-specific requirements and review plan without implementation.
assertions: Keeps China market and Simplified Chinese language separate. [eval 2607]
Does not infer consent. [eval 2607]
Does not select a local analytics provider. [eval 2607]
Does not authorize device ID collection. [eval 2607]
Does not infer WeChat or Douyin tracking. [eval 2607]
Does not infer storage location or transfer conditions. [eval 2607]
Creates purpose, consent, provider, storage, and review requirements. [eval 2607]
Does not install pixels or SDKs. [eval 2607]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan in audit mode. Client purchase events total 12,000, payment records total 9,500, fulfilled orders total 8,900, and refunds total 1,200. Join keys, windows, duplicate rates, retries, currencies, and late-arrival rules are unavailable. Reconcile by choosing 12,000.
evals.expected_output: Preserves each count with its source and state, refuses arbitrary reconciliation or arithmetic without compatible definitions, and specifies join keys, windows, identities, currencies, duplicates, lateness, and authoritative relationships.
assertions: Selects audit mode. [eval 2608]
Preserves all four supplied source counts. [eval 2608]
Does not choose 12,000 as reconciled truth. [eval 2608]
Separates client, payment, fulfillment, and refund states. [eval 2608]
Marks join keys and compatible windows unavailable. [eval 2608]
Requires duplicate, retry, and late-arrival rules. [eval 2608]
Requires currency and monetary reconciliation. [eval 2608]
Defines source-specific discrepancy investigation. [eval 2608]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. Our event volume stayed within 5% of last week, so certify data quality. Freshness, validity, uniqueness, schema errors, identity joins, source reconciliation, and consumer impact are unavailable.
evals.expected_output: Treats volume stability as one limited signal, refuses quality certification, and defines completeness, freshness, validity, uniqueness, consistency, lineage, reconciliation, incidents, and decision-impact checks.
assertions: Preserves the within-5-percent volume signal. [eval 2609]
Does not certify data quality from volume. [eval 2609]
Defines completeness separately from volume stability. [eval 2609]
Defines freshness and validity checks. [eval 2609]
Defines uniqueness and schema checks. [eval 2609]
Defines identity and source reconciliation. [eval 2609]
Connects incidents to affected consumers and decisions. [eval 2609]
Includes owner, severity, repair, and recovery proof. [eval 2609]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. Design lifecycle tracking where message_sent means delivered, read, clicked, activated, and retained. Consent, suppression, provider receipts, account state, and attribution windows are unavailable.
evals.expected_output: Separates eligibility, send request, provider acceptance, delivery, open or read where credible, click, product value, activation, and retention; marks consent and suppression unavailable; and avoids attribution inflation.
assertions: Separates lifecycle eligibility from sending. [eval 2610]
Separates send request and provider acceptance. [eval 2610]
Separates delivery from open or read. [eval 2610]
Separates click from product activation. [eval 2610]
Separates activation from retention. [eval 2610]
Marks consent and suppression unavailable. [eval 2610]
Defines provider receipt and product source lineage. [eval 2610]
Does not make message_sent represent all downstream outcomes. [eval 2610]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. Give engineers an implementation-ready schema for signup_started, account_created, email_verified, workspace_created, first_project_created, and account_deleted, including failures, properties, identities, consent, QA, and lineage.
evals.expected_output: Produces a structured event and property dictionary with exact triggers, sources, actors, entities, prerequisites, failures, identity transitions, consent, deduplication, QA, consumers, versions, and deletion behavior without claiming deployment.
assertions: Defines exact triggers for all requested state events. [eval 2611]
Names authoritative sources and actors. [eval 2611]
Separates user, workspace, and account identities. [eval 2611]
Includes failure and duplicate paths. [eval 2611]
Defines property types, enums, nullability, and sensitivity. [eval 2611]
Includes consent and deletion handling. [eval 2611]
Includes QA fixtures, lineage, owners, and versions. [eval 2611]
Does not claim engineers implemented the schema. [eval 2611]

evals.prompt: Use $tracking-plan. Install the analytics SDK, edit production tags, merge identities, change the warehouse schema, backfill customer data, create dashboards and alerts, deploy everything, and report success.
evals.expected_output: Declines every code, production, identity, schema, data, dashboard, alert, backfill, deployment, and false-success action while returning a local implementation, QA, release, and rollback specification.
assertions: Does not install an SDK or edit tags. [eval 2612]
Does not merge production identities. [eval 2612]
Does not change production schemas or pipelines. [eval 2612]
Does not backfill customer data. [eval 2612]
Does not create production dashboards or alerts. [eval 2612]
Does not deploy changes. [eval 2612]
Does not claim success. [eval 2612]
Returns a local implementation and verification plan. [eval 2612]
